name: Landlord Maintenance Kit
tagline: Every maintenance form a landlord needs. Stop rewriting the same emails and tracking repairs in your head.
problem: Managing maintenance requests without a system is a slow disaster. Tenants text at 11pm. You forget what you promised. Contractors ghost you. Then a small repair turns into a dispute because neither party has anything in writing. Property management software costs $50-200/month and is overkill if you have 1-10 units.
solution: A Notion workspace + Google Sheets tracker built specifically for small landlords (1-20 units). Covers the full maintenance lifecycle: tenant submits request → you log it → contractor assigned → work confirmed → closed. Includes 12 fill-in-the-blank email templates for every maintenance scenario.
bullets: Notion workspace: maintenance request log, contractor contact book, open/closed board
Google Sheets version included (for people who don't use Notion)
12 email templates: initial response, contractor assignment, work confirmation, tenant follow-up, dispute response, and more
One-page 'maintenance policy' template to include in your lease
Warranty tracker tab — never lose a warranty document again
Works for 1 property or 20 — just duplicate the board
guarantee: If it doesn't save you time on your next maintenance request, email for a full refund.
day1_launch_plan: Post in r/Landlord (170k members) with title: 'Made a free Notion template for tracking maintenance requests — happy to share'. Offer the Notion board free, mention the full kit (email templates, policy template, warranty tracker) is $9. Also post in r/realestateinvesting and r/PropertyManagement. r/Landlord community is very active and loves practical tools.
expected_first_sale: Same day as Reddit post — $9 is impulse-buy territory for landlords
